title: Saptakoteshwar Temple
tagline: Saptakoteshwar Temple — spiritual town of Goa

overview.short: Saptakoteshwar Temple is a Hindu temple, dedicated to Lord shiva at Narve in Goa, India, is considered to be one of the six great sites of temples of Shiva in the Konkan area.
overview.description: Saptakoteshwar Temple is a Hindu temple, dedicated to Lord shiva at Narve in Goa, India, is considered to be one of the six great sites of temples of Shiva in the Konkan area.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Naroa, Bicholim, Goa
topPlaces.description: Naroa is a village in Goa, India, approximately 35 kilometres from Panjim. It is the site of Saptakoteshwar Temple.

topPlaces.name: Mayem Lake
topPlaces.description: Mayem Lake is a freshwater lake located on the Indian state of Goa. It is situated in Bicholim taluka close to east of Mapusa town.

topPlaces.name: St Estevam
topPlaces.description: St Estevam (Santo Estêvão) is an estuarine islet in the Tiswadi taluka, Goa state, India. St Estevam is commonly referred as Jūvã (Isle) and has earned the monicker Šākêchô Jūvõ—the island of vegetables—famed for its long, seven-ridged, light green ladyfingers.

topPlaces.name: Fort Santo Estevão
topPlaces.description: Fort Santo Estevão (Fortaleza de Santo Estevão in Portuguese) is a military structure erected in Santo Estevão Island, in Goa, by the Portuguese. Built on the highest point of Santo Estevão Island, it was originally built as a watchtower to guard the Mandovi River, in 1550.

topPlaces.name: Piedade, Goa
topPlaces.description: Piedade is an island village located in Ilhas (also known as Tiswadi), a northern district in the state of Goa, India, and named after Our Lady of Compassion. The name comes from the Portuguese word piedade, which means "compassion".

topPlaces.name: Vanxim
topPlaces.description: Vanxim or Capão is an island of Goa situated in the Ilhas region. It can be reached by ferry from Divar.

topPlaces.name: Maem
topPlaces.description: Maem is a village located in the North Goa district in the state of Goa, India. In 2001, the population was 7544 of which 3799 were male and 3745 were female.

topPlaces.name: Naroa, Ilhas, Goa
topPlaces.description: Naroa is a village located on the eastern tip of the island of Divar, in the Indian state of Goa. It has a population of barely a thousand people, with about 80 houses.

items.note: Naroa is a village in Goa, India, approximately 35 kilometres from Panjim. It is the site of Saptakoteshwar Temple.
Mayem Lake is a freshwater lake located on the Indian state of Goa. It is situated in Bicholim taluka close to east of Mapusa town.

St Estevam (Santo Estêvão) is an estuarine islet in the Tiswadi taluka, Goa state, India. St Estevam is commonly referred as Jūvã (Isle) and has earned the monicker Šākêchô Jūvõ—the island of vegetables—famed for its long, seven-ridged, light green ladyfingers.

Fort Santo Estevão (Fortaleza de Santo Estevão in Portuguese) is a military structure erected in Santo Estevão Island, in Goa, by the Portuguese. Built on the highest point of Santo Estevão Island, it was originally built as a watchtower to guard the Mandovi River, in 1550.

Piedade is an island village located in Ilhas (also known as Tiswadi), a northern district in the state of Goa, India, and named after Our Lady of Compassion. The name comes from the Portuguese word piedade, which means "compassion".
Vanxim or Capão is an island of Goa situated in the Ilhas region. It can be reached by ferry from Divar.
Maem is a village located in the North Goa district in the state of Goa, India. In 2001, the population was 7544 of which 3799 were male and 3745 were female.
Naroa is a village located on the eastern tip of the island of Divar, in the Indian state of Goa. It has a population of barely a thousand people, with about 80 houses.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Saptakoteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Saptakoteshwar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Saptakoteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Manohar International Airport, Mopa (North Goa) (22 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (22 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Saptakoteshwar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Saptakoteshwar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Saptakoteshwar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Saptakoteshwar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Saptakoteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Naroa, Bicholim, Goa, Mayem Lake, St Estevam, Fort Santo Estevão, Piedade, Goa.
